items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution (CVE-2026-48358, CVSS 10.0) via improper output encoding in webhooks and privilege escalation (CVE-2026-48356, CVSS 9.6) via unrestricted file upload.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Auth Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: An Incorrect Authorization vulnerability (CVSS 7.5) allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to Adobe Commerce.

items.title: Adobe Commerce DOS Vulnerability
items.summary: A denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce, caused by a vulnerable third-party component, can be triggered remotely without authentication or user interaction, leading to application crashes.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This bulletin, updated in May 2026, addresses critical and important vulnerabilities from a June 2025 update, including security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and arbitrary code execution, notably CVE-2025-47110 for stored XSS via server-side template injection.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update protects against multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and gain unauthorized access to store data.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique to bypass validation. Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.
